Package com.stevenlagoy.presidency.util

Class StringOperations

java.lang.Object
com.stevenlagoy.presidency.util.StringOperations
public final class StringOperations extends Object

  • Method Details

    • isInString

      public static boolean isInString(String line, int position)
      Determines whether a given position in a line of text is currently inside a string literal, accounting for escaped quotes.

      This method scans the input line from the beginning up to (but not including) the specified position. It toggles the inString flag each time it encounters a non-escaped double quote character. This helps determine whether the character at the given position falls within a string.

      Parameters:
      line - The input string to analyze.
      position - The index position in the string to check.
      Returns:
      true if the position is within a string literal, false otherwise.

    • isInArray

      public static boolean isInArray(String line, int position)
      Determines whether the specified position in a line of text is currently inside a JSON array.

      This method traverses the line up to (but not including) the given position, tracking array nesting depth. It increments depth when encountering a non-escaped '[' character outside of a string, and decrements it for ']'. If the resulting depth is greater than 0 at the given position, the position is considered to be inside an array.

      Parameters:
      line - The input string to analyze.
      position - The index position in the string to check.
      Returns:
      true if the position is within a JSON array, false otherwise.
      See Also:
      • isInString(String, int)

    • isInObject

      public static boolean isInObject(String line, int position)

    • containsUnquotedChar

      public static boolean containsUnquotedChar(String line, char target)
      Checks if the given line contains at least one unquoted occurrence of the target character.

      This method ignores characters that appear inside string literals (delimited by double quotes) and only considers unescaped characters outside of strings.

      Parameters:
      line - The string to search.
      target - The character to look for.
      Returns:
      true if the character appears outside of a string, false otherwise.
      See Also:
      • countUnquotedChar(String, char)
      • isInString(String, int)

    • countUnquotedChar

      public static int countUnquotedChar(String line, char target)
      Counts how many times a target character appears outside of string literals in the given line.

      This method scans the string and increments a counter for each instance of the target character that is not within a quoted string. Escaped quotes are correctly ignored.

      Parameters:
      line - The string to analyze.
      target - The char to count.
      Returns:
      The number of unquoted occurrences of the target character.
      See Also:
      • isInString(String, int)

    • findFirstUnquotedChar

      public static int findFirstUnquotedChar(String line, char target)
      Finds the first instance of the target character outside of a string literal.
      Parameters:
      line - The string through which to search
      target - The char to search for
      Returns:
      The index of the first unquoted instance of the target character, or -1 if not found
      See Also:
      • isInString(String, int)

    • splitByUnquotedString

      public static String[] splitByUnquotedString(String string, String separator)
      Splits the input string around occurrences of the given separator string, ignoring separators that appear inside quoted strings.

      This method respects string literals delimited by double quotes, treating escaped quotes correctly. Trims whitespace around each resulting substring.

      Parameters:
      string - The input string to split.
      separator - The substring to split on, when not within quotes.
      Returns:
      An array of trimmed substrings resulting from the split.
      See Also:
      • isInString(String, int)

    • splitByUnquotedString

      public static String[] splitByUnquotedString(String string, String separator, int limit)
      Splits the input string around occurrences of the given separator string, ignoring separators that appear inside quoted strings, and limits the number of resulting substrings.

      Trims whitespace around each substring. Stops splitting once limit parts have been collected, placing the remainder into the last entry.

      Parameters:
      string - The input string to split.
      separator - The substring to split on, when not within quotes.
      limit - The maximum number of substrings to return. Must be at least 1.
      Returns:
      An array of trimmed substrings resulting from the split, with at most limit entries.
      See Also:
      • isInString(String, int)

    • splitByStringNotInArray

      public static String[] splitByStringNotInArray(String string, String separator)
      Splits the input string around occurrences of the given separator string, ignoring separators that appear within array brackets [...].

      This method treats brackets as array delimiters and avoids splitting inside them, even if the separator appears. Trims whitespace around each resulting substring.

      Parameters:
      string - The input string to split.
      separator - The substring to split on, when not within array brackets.
      Returns:
      An array of trimmed substrings resulting from the split.
      See Also:
      • isInArray(String, int)

    • splitByStringNotInObject

      public static String[] splitByStringNotInObject(String string, String separator)

    • splitByStringNotNested

      public static String[] splitByStringNotNested(String string, String separator)
      Splits a string by a separator only when it is not inside an object or an array.
      Parameters:
      string -
      separator -
      Returns:

    • replaceLast

      public static String replaceLast(String text, String regex, String replacement)
      Replaces the last occurrence of a specified regular expression with a replacement string in the input text. This method uses the replaceFirst(String, String, String) method by reversing the string and performing the replacement on the first occurrence in the reversed string.
      Parameters:
      text - The input string in which the replacement will be made.
      regex - The regular expression to match for the replacement.
      replacement - The string to replace the matched regex.
      Returns:
      A new string with the last occurrence of the regex replaced by the replacement string.
      See Also:
      • String.replaceFirst(String, String)

    • replaceFirst

      public static String replaceFirst(String text, String regex, String replacement)
      Replaces the first occurrence of a specified regular expression with a replacement string in the input text. This method reverses the input string, performs the replacement on the first occurrence of the regex in the reversed string, and then reverses the resulting string again to produce the final output.
      Parameters:
      text - The input string in which the replacement will be made.
      regex - The regular expression to match for the replacement
      replacement - The string to replace the matched regex
      Returns:
      A new string with the first occurrence of the regex replaced by the replacement string.
      See Also:
      • String.replaceFirst(String, String)

    • replaceAll

      public static String replaceAll(String text, String regex, String replacement)
      Replaces all occurrences of a specified regular expression in the input text with a replacement string.
      Parameters:
      text - The input string in which the replacements will be made.
      regex - The regular expression to match for the replacement.
      replacement - The string to replace the matched regex
      Returns:
      A new string with all occurrences of the regex replaced by the replacement string.
      See Also:
      • String.replaceAll(String, String)

    • replaceAllNotInString

      public static String replaceAllNotInString(String text, String regex, String replacement)
      Replaces all occurrences of a specified regular expression where it appears outside of a quotated string in the input text with a replacement string.
      Parameters:
      text - The input string in which the replacements will be made.
      regex - The regular expression to match for the replacement.
      replacement - The string to replace the matched regex
      Returns:
      A new string with all occurrences of the regex outside of a string replaced by the replacement string
      See Also:
      • isInString(String, int)

    • replaceFirstCount

      public static String replaceFirstCount(String text, String regex, String replacement, int count)
      Replaces the first count occurrences of the specified regular expression with the replacement string in the input text.
      Parameters:
      text - The input string in which the replacements will be made.
      regex - The regular expression to match for the replacement.
      replacement - The string to replace the matched regex.
      count - The number of occurrences to replace.
      Returns:
      A new string with the first count occurrences of the regex replaced by the replacement string.
      See Also:
      • replaceFirst(String, String, String)

    • replaceLastCount

      public static String replaceLastCount(String text, String regex, String replacement, int count)
      Replaces the last count occurrences of the specified regular expression with the replacement string in the input text.
      Parameters:
      text - The input string in which the replacements will be made.
      regex - The regular expression to match for the replacement.
      replacement - The string to replace the matched regex.
      count - The number of occurrences to replace.
      Returns:
      A new string with the last count occurrences of the regex replaced by the replacement string.
      See Also:
      • replaceLast(String, String, String)
